canonical_name: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Care Scheme (Sikkim)

local_names.pa: ਮੁੱਖ ਮੰਤਰੀ ਵਿਆਪਕ ਸਿਹਤ ਸੰਭਾਲ ਯੋਜਨਾ (ਸਿੱਕਮ)
local_names.en: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Care Scheme (Sikkim)

exclusion_rules.description: Only residents of Sikkim are eligible for CMCHCS
Must hold a valid Sikkim Subject Certificate (SSC) to be eligible. Non-SSC holders are excluded.

family_criteria.family_definition: All family members listed under the Sikkim Subject Certificate are covered. Coverage is universal for the entire household.

covered_procedures: Free treatment at all Sikkim government hospitals
Free OPD (Out-Patient Department) consultation at government facilities
Free hospitalization at government hospitals (room, nursing, ICU)
Referral to empanelled private hospitals for specialized care not available in government facilities
Cardiology (bypass surgery, angioplasty, valve replacement at referral hospitals)
Oncology (chemotherapy, radiation therapy, surgical oncology at referral hospitals)
Nephrology (dialysis, renal care at referral hospitals)
Neurosurgery (brain and spinal procedures at referral hospitals)
Orthopaedic (knee replacement, hip replacement, fracture management)
Diagnostics and lab tests at government facilities
Medicines and consumables at government hospitals
excluded_procedures: Treatment at non-empanelled private hospitals without referral
Drug rehabilitation and de-addiction (unless at government facility)
Treatment sought outside Sikkim without prior government referral

required_documents.name: Sikkim Subject Certificate (SSC)
required_documents.notes: Primary eligibility document. Must be a valid Sikkim Subject Certificate issued by the Government of Sikkim. This is mandatory with no alternatives.

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Required for identity verification at government and empanelled hospitals

required_documents.name: Any Government-issued Photo ID
required_documents.local_name_hi: कोई भी सरकारी फोटो पहचान पत्र
required_documents.notes: Additional identity proof for verification if needed

enrollment_channels: Automatic for all Sikkim Subject Certificate holders
Primary Health Centres (PHC) and Community Health Centres (CHC)
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Enrollment is automatic for all holders of Sikkim Subject Certificate (SSC). No separate registration required.
Step 2: Visit any government hospital in Sikkim with your Sikkim Subject Certificate and Aadhaar card.
Step 3: Hospital verifies SSC and provides free treatment at the government facility.
Step 4: If specialized care is needed that is not available at the government hospital, the hospital issues a referral to an empanelled private hospital.
Step 5: Present the referral letter, SSC, and Aadhaar at the empanelled private hospital for cashless specialized treatment.
Step 6: For emergencies, present SSC at any government hospital for immediate free treatment.
description_for_embedding: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Care Scheme (CMCHCS) is Sikkim's universal healthcare scheme providing free medical treatment to all residents holding a Sikkim Subject Certificate (SSC). Unlike insurance-based schemes, CMCHCS provides universal free healthcare at the point of service with no coverage cap, no income restriction, and no premium. All Sikkim residents with a valid SSC receive free treatment at government hospitals across the state. For specialized care not available at government facilities, patients are referred to empanelled private hospitals at no cost. The scheme covers OPD consultations, hospitalization, surgery, diagnostics, medicines, and emergency care at government hospitals. Enrollment is automatic for SSC holders with no separate registration needed. The only hard requirement is possessing a valid Sikkim Subject Certificate. Helpline number is 104.
